title: Weight loss GI documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.
red_flags.rationale: The exact colorectal section supports clinician assessment of bowel-habit change, rectal bleeding, abdominal symptoms, anaemia, and weight-loss concern without diagnosing cancer.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

investigations.text: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.
investigations.rationale: The exact screening section supports duration, measured or reported weight loss, appetite, intake, swallowing, bowel symptoms, illness, and functional impact without assigning malnutrition or cause.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

source.title: Nutrition support for adults: oral nutrition support, enteral tube feeding and parenteral nutrition — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — screening, clinical concern, weight loss, intake, and swallowing

investigations.text: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports recording existing laboratory, imaging, endoscopy, nutrition-risk, and clinician-entered plan context without generating tests or nutrition support.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.

source_limitations: Measured weight, percentage and timeframe, intake, swallowing, bowel symptoms, systemic context, cause, diagnosis, tests, nutrition support, referral, urgency, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
